figures.caption: Figure 4: QQ plots and histograms (with fitted Gaussian densities) of the standardized statistic Sn for two representative configurations.

Figure 3: Empirical correlation matrices with dependence parameter θ = 0.65 in the Gaussian AR(1) input time series. The numbering of the output neurons has been chosen to maximize the correlation of successive neurons.

Table 1: Completed simulation by dataset. For each dataset, the best mean accuracy among GAP, WGAP, and reg WGAP is highlighted in red, while the second-best is highlighted in orange. The last column reports the median selected regularization parameter λ across the five runs. Each method is trained for 200 epochs using the same batch size, while the regularized variant reg WGAP selects λ by 5-fold cross-validation, with 80 training epochs in each fold.

Figure 2: Empirical autocorrelation matrices R̂GAP(θ) for the one-layer GAP network with m1 = 10 under fixed Gaussian initialization and input generated from an AR(1) process with parameters θ = 0 (left panel) and θ = 0.99 (right panel). The numbering of the output neurons has been chosen to maximize the correlation of successive neurons, thereby revealing the strong clustering of the outputs.
Figure 1: FCN architecture with Residual connection.
